## What Changed

- Added adaptive capture diagnostics so low-quality browser and snapshot captures report clearer fallback reasons and session context
- Added policy signal origin reporting and richer session diagnostics to the CLI, runtime contracts, and serve surfaces
- Improved browser-backed fallback heuristics for shell-heavy and JavaScript-heavy pages, and aligned adapter resolution so local and CI follow the same execution path
- Stabilized env-dependent CLI and serve-daemon gate tests, reduced the required CI gate to product-risk checks, and fixed the Sonar quality gate regressions on `main`
- Added the AWS page type benchmark generator and its supporting spec so browser capture quality can be measured against a broader page mix

## User Impact

- Browser-backed `open`, `read`, and `extract` flows should recover more predictably on dynamic pages that previously looked like app shells or sparse placeholders
- Policy and capture outputs now expose more diagnostic detail, which makes review, fallback, and debugging decisions easier to trace
- No new top-level user command was added in this release; this version focuses on reliability, diagnostics, and release pipeline stability

## Notes

- This release follows `v0.1.5` and keeps the same tag-driven standalone bundle publishing workflow
- The official install and update path remains the GitHub Release bundle for the matching release tag
